response: An important leader of the Democratic-Republican Party was Thomas Jefferson, who formed the party in 1791–93 to oppose the centralizing policies of the Federalist Party. He was also a key figure in the party, serving as its leader and playing a significant role in the 1800 presidential election where he defeated President John Adams. Additionally, James Madison, another prominent Democratic-Republican, served as Secretary of State and was a central figure in the party's governance. Albert Gallatin, a Swiss-American politician and diplomat, was also an important leader of the Democratic-Republican Party, serving as Secretary of the Treasury and representing Pennsylvania in the Senate and House of Representatives.
